title: podman remote cp
body:
support podman cp on the remote. This enables copy file/folder between remote container and Podman nodes

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:

@baude Honestly I think we should have separate endpoints for copying into the container, and copying out of the container... we can share the code on the backend if we want, but it gets really confusing determining how copy works otherwise IMO

> @baude Honestly I think we should have separate endpoints for copying into the container, and copying out of the container... we can share the code on the backend if we want, but it gets really confusing determining how copy works otherwise IMO

thats fine, i dont care where the logic lives.

inside this function [func (r *LocalRuntime) Copy](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3327/files#diff-4d0250b516fbac4efaf286206cfa3d3fR1171) I called functions that uses the varlink API `SendFileOverVarlink`, and `GetFileFromRemoteHost`, I think this follows the comment above. 

But the file was sent to /tmp, I want to move/untar it to the path inside the container, and also need to do `os.Stat()` to the path related with the remote, I don't kown how to make this so I added two the varlink APIs to help this. How can I make it better?
